---
title: "nitrix.geometry.transform"
generated: true
signature-hash: e6a2cc41aec5e086
description: "Lie-group parametrisation of rigid and affine transforms."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-transform-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-transform/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.transform`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Lie-group parametrisation of rigid and affine transforms.

Registration is optimisation on a transform group; this module supplies
the chart between a flat parameter vector (what the Gauss-Newton /
Levenberg-Marquardt optimiser moves) and a homogeneous transform matrix
(what [`affine_grid`](nitrix-geometry-transform/affine_grid.qmd) + [`spatial_transform`](nitrix-geometry-grid/spatial_transform.qmd) apply).

- **Rigid** ([`rigid_exp`](nitrix-geometry-transform/rigid_exp.qmd) / [`rigid_log`](nitrix-geometry-transform/rigid_log.qmd)) -- rotation from the
  axis-angle ($\mathfrak{so}(n)$) exponential in **closed form**
  (Rodrigues in 3-D, the planar rotation in 2-D) plus a **direct**
  translation.  Closed-form rotation is GPU-native (no cuSolver), so the
  rigid optimiser's hot loop stays on-device.  Parameter order: 3-D
  $(\omega_x, \omega_y, \omega_z, t_x, t_y, t_z)$ (6); 2-D
  $(\theta, t_x, t_y)$ (3).
- **Affine** ([`affine_exp`](nitrix-geometry-transform/affine_exp.qmd)) -- the linear block is
  $\exp(A)$ of a general $\mathfrak{gl}(n)$ generator
  (guaranteeing an invertible, orientation-preserving $\det > 0$
  map) plus a direct translation.  Parameter order: $n^2$ linear
  entries (row-major $A$) then $n$ translation.  Uses
  `matrix_exp`.

[`apply_affine`](nitrix-geometry-transform/apply_affine.qmd) / [`affine_grid`](nitrix-geometry-transform/affine_grid.qmd) turn a homogeneous matrix into
the absolute sample coordinates [`spatial_transform`](nitrix-geometry-grid/spatial_transform.qmd) consumes, with
an optional rotation/scaling `center` (registration rotates about the
image centre, not the voxel origin).

Coordinates are in index space, matching [`identity_grid`](nitrix-geometry-grid/identity_grid.qmd)
(`grid[i] == i`).  Supported spatial ranks: 2 and 3.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `d1` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-transform-symbols}
:::
